column65: Thanks to the organisers, it was a great event! (only complaint was the small dinner)
Hope to meet up the next time.

Compared to the poster space used at the Crick in 2024, the poster space at the Wellcome Collection was very cramped. Whenever the person next to me was explaining their poster to somebody, they blocked view of my poster and vice-versa.
In general, I think it is a good idea to have exhibitors in the same space as posters, but this definitely requires a bigger room.

column4: Physics/Biophysics;Image Analysis/Processing;Computer Science;Software Engineering;Machine Learning;Computer Vision;
column65: As always, I had a great time at CBIAS and look forward to CBIAS 2026!

I enjoyed a lot and, even though i didn't go to the social for personal reasons, there was plenty of opportunity for netwroking and asking questions. The poster room was tiny and a bit  uncomfortable, but that's what you have available
As a suggestion, there could be two poster sessions. One for odd-number posters and one for even numbers. The poster session was too crowded and difficult to navigate the various posters.

column4: Cell/Molecular Biology;Developmental Biology;Image Analysis/Processing;Software Engineering;Machine Learning;

column65: The level of noise and the lighting at the social event were far from optimal.
The evening social didn't have enough food

i wish the poster session was a separate event to lunch to give people more time. I also thought flash talks should have either been a bit longer or have all posters do a flash talk - otherwsie all good but the poster room was a little crowded
The only issue for me was the lack of tables and space to have coffee and (more so) lunch.
Looking forward to next year!
I enjoyed everything apart from the evening social. I didn’t think it was accessible with not enough seats for everyone, a loud & dark environment and food without sufficient allergy labelling

- Room for the poster session was too small
- For international participants, 2 days is very short. Consider allowing more time for poster sessions in addition to a few more (early carreer) talks, and make it 3 days
- I would prefer having it at the Crick, also for us to see the institute
- Overall, I very enjoyed the conference!

As an exhibitor, not enough effort was made to get delegates to interact with sponsors stands. I understand the change of venue made this a challenge, but whenever tea and coffee isn't in the same room as the sponsors it presents a real challenge getting interaction. Delegates quite often don't realise our sponsorship payments helps contribute to the running of these events, so need encouragement to come and talk to the sponsors. In future, if not in the usual place, I would suggest somehow arranging coffee/tea & lunch being in the same room as the sponsors
Love coming every year. I like that students/ECR get to mingle with "big shots" as the event is not massive and plenty of time for networking.
Didn't enjoy the social venue because it was dark and crowded. Otherwise an excellent conference in all other aspects, thanks so much to all who put in the time and effort to organise it!
Please keep it as is - I usually like something like a social speed networking (to help shy participants reach out to further participants).
As an international guest, it would be more easily worth the travel time to have one extra day. Also, I would appreciate a slightly stronger delineation between poster session and lunch time.

Great symposium! The only minor comments I have are that coffee/tea would have been appreciated at the beginning of the second day and that the food was a bit disorganized at the social. Otherwise wonderful and I would like to attend the next one.
Thank you for streaming online, it was great to be able to see the talks if not able to attend in person.

In my opinion the panel discussions could be structured a bit more to increase the discussion, e.g. with statements where some of the panelists agree and some disagree. Now it was more like an informative session where everybody agreed and told their own experiences.

I gave a low grade to the social because in the pub it was too hot, the room was too small with a narrow ceiling and there was only one type of bier served, with a weird system where there was beer on the floor and the food was served quite slowly, so at the end I decided to leave earlier than I would have stayed if the place was nicer.

For the program, I would have preferred to have only one panel discussion and give more space to talks.
